page.caption: High-temperature corrosion kinetics analyzer — FeCrAl / sol-gel coatings

sidebar.demo_data: Using demo data (Table 9.1 from the Master's thesis).

dashboard.theory_body:
**Tammann's law** describes parabolic metal oxidation kinetics: \(x^2 = k \cdot t\),
where \(x\) is e.g. mass gain or scale thickness. CorrosionLab automates
calculations from equations (9.1)–(9.4) of the Master's thesis:

- **9.1** — relative mass change Δm/m₀ [%]
- **9.2** — protective effectiveness Sₖ vs. uncoated alloy
- **9.3** — scale thickness h [μm]
- **9.4** — parabolic rate constant kₚ

spallation.description: The algorithm analyzes the first mass difference between consecutive cycles. A negative mass gain indicates potential spallation (e.g. TM4B, TM5A @ 96 h).
spallation.no_alerts: No spallation detected in selected samples.

extrapolation.no_samples: Select samples to analyze in the sidebar.

expert.intro: Recommendations based on conclusions from the Master's thesis (ch. 11, pp. 49–50).

max_protection.title: Maximum protection — ZrO₂ (5 layers)
max_protection.description: A five-layer coating with yttria-stabilized ZrO₂ nanoparticles achieved ~100% protective effectiveness (conclusion C, sample TM4B).
rules: C: SiO₂ matrix with ZrO₂ nanoparticles shows highest protective effectiveness.

min_spallation.title: Minimum spallation — avoid Y₂O₃, prefer ZrO₂
min_spallation.description: Coatings with Y₂O₃ (TM5A/TM5B) show spallation and lower effectiveness. ZrO₂ in SiO₂ matrix (5 layers) gives the best scale stability.
rules: F: Y₂O₃ increases spallation risk; ZrO₂ improves high-temperature resistance.
D: Five-layer coatings generally outperform single-layer variants.

low_cost.title: Low cost — single-layer SiO₂ (Aerosil 380)
low_cost.description: Single-layer SiO₂ with Aerosil 380 (TM2A) provides ~55% effectiveness with simpler application and lower cost.
rules: B: Sol-gel coatings are easy to apply without costly equipment.
D: Single-layer coatings are cheaper but less effective.

multi_layer.title: Multi-layer protection — 5 layers ZrO₂
multi_layer.description: Rule D: five-layer coatings provide better protection. TM4B (ZrO₂, 5 layers) — ~80–100% effectiveness.
rules: D: Five-layer coatings provide better protection than single-layer SiO₂.
H: Reactive metal oxide nanoparticles reach 27–100% effectiveness.

single_layer.title: Single layer — SiO₂ Aerosil 380
single_layer.description: Compromise between cost and effectiveness: TM2A (~55% S_k).
rules: H: Single-layer reactive oxides still outperform Al₂O₃/SiO₂ fillers.

balanced.title: Balanced recommendation — CeO₂/ZrO₂ (5 layers)
balanced.description: Coating TM7B (CeO₂/ZrO₂, 5 layers) combines moderate effectiveness (~40%) with good scale morphology — a cost-protection compromise.
rules: G: Nanoparticle type affects scale morphology and oxidation rate.
H: Reactive metal oxides (CeO₂/ZrO₂) reach moderate-to-high effectiveness.

errors.fitting_min_points: At least two measurement points are required for fitting.
